Project2 Data Mining

Group 6: Cristhian Cruz, Julian McKinley, Luisa Ribeiro, Matheus Lucena

2026-03-03

Project part 2

Setup and data cleaning

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(caret)
  library(MASS)
  library(leaps)
  library(glmnet)
  library(dplyr)
})

set.seed(5703)
df_raw <- read.csv("kc_house_data.csv")
# Convert likely categorical predictors
df <- df_raw %>%
  mutate(
    zipcode = if ("zipcode" %in% names(.)) as.factor(zipcode) else NULL,
    waterfront = if ("waterfront" %in% names(.)) as.factor(waterfront) else NULL,
    view = if ("view" %in% names(.)) as.factor(view) else NULL,
    condition = if ("condition" %in% names(.)) as.factor(condition) else NULL,
    grade = if ("grade" %in% names(.)) as.factor(grade) else NULL
  )

# We will be using log(price) for final model
df <- df %>%
  mutate(log_price = log(price)) %>%
  dplyr::select(-price)
# Check for variables with perfect multicollinearity 
alias_obj <- alias(lm(log_price ~ ., data = df))
redundant_cols <- rownames(alias_obj$Complete)
redundant_cols
## [1] "sqft_basement"
# Drop ID, Date + redundant variables from modeling
drop_cols <- intersect(names(df), c("id", "date", redundant_cols))
df_model <- dplyr::select(df, -dplyr::all_of(drop_cols))
# Train/Test Split
set.seed(5703)
idx <- createDataPartition(df_model$log_price, p = 0.80, list = FALSE)
train_df <- df_model[idx, , drop = FALSE]
test_df <- df_model[-idx, , drop = FALSE]

cat("Train rows:", nrow(train_df), "\nTest rows:", nrow(test_df), "\n")
## Train rows: 17292 
## Test rows: 4321

Part 1: Model selection methods

A. Best Subset Selection

zipcode is not included in best subset selection because it has 70 levels once dummy encoded. This results in too many predictors for regsubsets to handle.

# get the number of predictors after dummy encoding categorical predictors
p <- ncol(model.matrix(~ . - log_price -zipcode, data=train_df))

best_fit <- regsubsets(log_price ~ . - zipcode, data = train_df, nvmax = p)

best_summary <-summary(best_fit)

# View metrics
cat("Regsubsets Metrics\nAdjusted R^2:\n")
best_summary$adjr2
cat("\nCP:\n")
best_summary$cp
cat("\nBIC:\n")
best_summary$bic

# Identify optimal model size for each criterion
best_adjr2 <- which.max(best_summary$adjr2)
best_cp    <- which.min(best_summary$cp)
best_bic   <- which.min(best_summary$bic)

cat("\nIndexes of best subset for each metric:\nR^2: ", best_adjr2, "\n")
cat("Cp: ", best_cp, "\n")
cat("BIC: ", best_bic, "\n")
## Regsubsets Metrics
## Adjusted R^2:
##  [1] 0.4894943 0.6585804 0.6773973 0.6887758 0.7009284 0.7138421 0.7273636
##  [8] 0.7379228 0.7444998 0.7503619 0.7529463 0.7560636 0.7583000 0.7606340
## [15] 0.7624167 0.7640753 0.7658440 0.7674186 0.7689927 0.7697929 0.7705509
## [22] 0.7710392 0.7715463 0.7720173 0.7723531 0.7725347 0.7726220 0.7726319
## [29] 0.7726303 0.7726185 0.7726063 0.7725934
## 
## CP:
##  [1] 21526.37144  8671.05013  7241.03782  6376.64756  5453.52082  4472.64565
##  [7]  3445.68181  2643.98041  2144.99989  1700.41092  1504.92600  1268.97958
## [13]  1099.98884   923.60758   789.12101   664.08302   530.69115   412.05899
## [19]   293.48194   233.68977   177.11557   141.02308   103.51461    68.74226
## [25]    44.24332    31.45395    25.82794    26.08029    27.20177    29.09778
## [31]    31.02507    33.00000
## 
## BIC:
##  [1] -11607.82 -18555.45 -19526.99 -20139.16 -20819.14 -21573.64 -22401.90
##  [8] -23076.18 -23506.91 -23899.52 -24070.71 -24281.53 -24432.04 -24591.07
## [15] -24711.58 -24823.96 -24945.33 -25053.25 -25161.92 -25213.17 -25261.44
## [22] -25289.53 -25319.11 -25346.04 -25362.77 -25367.82 -25365.70 -25357.69
## [29] -25348.81 -25339.16 -25329.47 -25319.74
## 
## Indexes of best subset for each metric:
## R^2:  28 
## Cp:  27 
## BIC:  26
# Plots
par(mfrow = c(1,3))

plot(best_summary$adjr2, xlab = "Number of predictors", ylab = "Adjusted R2", type = "b")
points(best_adjr2, best_summary$adjr2[best_adjr2], col = "red", pch = 19)

plot(best_summary$cp, xlab = "Number of predictors", ylab = "Cp", type = "b")
points(best_cp, best_summary$cp[best_cp], col = "red", pch = 19)

plot(best_summary$bic, xlab = "Number of predictors", ylab = "BIC", type = "b")
points(best_bic, best_summary$bic[best_bic], col = "red", pch = 19)

par(mfrow = c(1,1))

Refitting the model using the best BIC subset. We’re using the all-or-nothing rule for categorical values. If one of the categorical levels is selected, the whole categorical variable is selected in the final model.

best_bic_coefs <- coef(best_fit, best_bic)
# Extract predictor names (remove intercept)
selected_vars <- names(best_bic_coefs)[-1]
all_original_vars <- colnames(train_df)
# Selected predictors might be a dummy-coded variable, so we need to map it back to its original variable name.
final_selection <- all_original_vars[sapply(all_original_vars, function(v) any(grepl(v, selected_vars)))]
cat("Best variable subset selected by regsubsets:\n")
selected_vars
cat("\n\nVariables selected for final model:\n")
final_selection
## Best variable subset selected by regsubsets:
##  [1] "bedrooms"      "bathrooms"     "sqft_living"   "sqft_lot"     
##  [5] "floors"        "waterfront1"   "view1"         "view2"        
##  [9] "view3"         "view4"         "condition3"    "condition4"   
## [13] "condition5"    "grade6"        "grade7"        "grade8"       
## [17] "grade9"        "grade10"       "grade11"       "grade12"      
## [21] "grade13"       "yr_built"      "yr_renovated"  "lat"          
## [25] "long"          "sqft_living15"
## 
## 
## Variables selected for final model:
##  [1] "bedrooms"      "bathrooms"     "sqft_living"   "sqft_lot"     
##  [5] "floors"        "waterfront"    "view"          "condition"    
##  [9] "grade"         "yr_built"      "yr_renovated"  "lat"          
## [13] "long"          "sqft_living15"
# Build formula dynamically
formula_bss <- as.formula(
  paste("log_price ~", paste(final_selection, collapse = " + "))
)

# Refit model using training data
bss_model <- lm(formula_bss, data = train_df)
summary(bss_model)

# Predictions on test set
pred_test <- predict(bss_model, newdata = test_df)

# Test RMSE
rmse_bss <- sqrt(mean((test_df$log_price - pred_test)^2))

# Test R-squared
r2_bss <- 1 - sum((test_df$log_price - pred_test)^2) /
  sum((test_df$log_price - mean(test_df$log_price))^2)

cat("Test set metric of model with best subset selection\n")
cat("RMSE: ", rmse_bss, "\nR^2: ", r2_bss)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = formula_bss, data = train_df)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1.69985 -0.16035  0.00094  0.15563  1.21607 
## 
## Coefficients:

## (Intercept)   -5.359e+01  2.176e+00 -24.633  < 2e-16 ***
## bedrooms      -1.361e-02  2.668e-03  -5.101 3.42e-07 ***
## bathrooms      7.026e-02  4.538e-03  15.483  < 2e-16 ***
## sqft_living    1.481e-04  4.774e-06  31.028  < 2e-16 ***

## 
## Test set metric of model with best subset selection
## RMSE:  0.2524546 
## R^2:  0.7606441

B. Stepwise Selection(Forward, Backward or Both)

full_model <- lm(log_price ~ ., data = train_df)

step_model <- step(full_model, direction = "both", trace = FALSE)
summary(step_model)

#top 10 significant predictors
top_vars <- broom::tidy(step_model) %>%
  filter(p.value < 0.01) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(statistic))) %>%
  head(10)

top_vars

# Predict on test set
pred_step <- predict(step_model, newdata = test_df)

# Test RMSE
rmse_step <- sqrt(mean((test_df$log_price - pred_step)^2))

# Test R^2
r2_step <- 1 - sum((test_df$log_price - pred_step)^2) /
           sum((test_df$log_price - mean(test_df$log_price))^2)

cat("Stepwise Test RMSE:", rmse_step, "\n")
cat("Stepwise Test R^2:", r2_step, "\n")
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = log_price ~ bathrooms + sqft_living + sqft_lot +

##     data = train_df)

## Stepwise Test RMSE: 0.1852195 
## Stepwise Test R^2: 0.87116

Part 2: Regularization and 5-fold cross validation

our outcome is continuous so we will be using ridge and lasso

# exclude price and log_price from predictors
x_train <- model.matrix(log_price ~ . - log_price, data = train_df)[, -1]
y_train <- train_df$log_price

x_test <- model.matrix(log_price ~ . - log_price, data = test_df)[, -1]
y_test <- test_df$log_price

# 5-fold CV for Ridge Regression
set.seed(5703)
cv_ridge <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 0, nfolds = 5)
plot(cv_ridge)
title("Ridge Regression CV Error Curve")

# Optimal lambdas
cv_ridge$lambda.min
cv_ridge$lambda.1se

# Ridge coefficients at lambda.min
coef(cv_ridge, s = "lambda.min")

ridge_pred <- predict(cv_ridge, s = "lambda.min", newx = x_test)
rmse_ridge <- sqrt(mean((y_test - ridge_pred)^2))
cat("Ridge Test RMSE:", rmse_ridge)

# 5-fold CV for LASSO Regression
set.seed(5703)
cv_lasso <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1, nfolds = 5)
plot(cv_lasso)
title("LASSO Regression CV Error Curve")

# Optimal lambdas
cv_lasso$lambda.min
cv_lasso$lambda.1se

# LASSO coefficients at lambda.min
lasso_coef <- coef(cv_lasso, s = "lambda.min")
lasso_coef

lasso_pred <- predict(cv_lasso, s = "lambda.min", newx = x_test)
rmse_lasso <- sqrt(mean((y_test - lasso_pred)^2))
cat("LASSO Test RMSE:", rmse_lasso)

# Which predictors are zero
zero_predictors <- rownames(lasso_coef)[lasso_coef[, 1] == 0]
zero_predictors

model_results <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Best Subset (BIC)", "Stepwise", "Ridge", "LASSO"),
  RMSE = c(rmse_bss, rmse_step, rmse_ridge, rmse_lasso)
)

model_results
